source.definition: A small invertebrate animal with six legs, often having wings.

explanation.sourceText: The English word 'insect' comes from the Latin 'insectum', meaning 'cut into sections', referring to the segmented body structure.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „insect” provine din latină, din 'insectum', care înseamnă 'tăiat în bucăți', referindu-se la corpul segmentat.

target.definition: Un animal mic fără coloană vertebrală, cu șase picioare, adesea cu aripi.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'insectă' was borrowed from French 'insecte', which in turn derives from Latin 'insectum'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „insectă” a fost preluat din franceză, „insecte”, care provine la rândul său din latină, „insectum”.

examples.sourceText: The butterfly is a beautiful insect.
Insects live in many different environments.
examples.targetText: Insectele trăiesc în multe medii diferite.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word 'insectă' sounds very similar to the English 'insect', making it easy to remember.
Notice that 'insectă' ends with '-ă', a common feminine noun ending in Romanian.
Both words come from Latin 'insectum', meaning 'cut into sections', which helps explain the segmented body of insects.
Think of an insect as having many small 'sections'—this can help you recall the meaning of 'insectă'.
